title: The idea
text: A design or event idea can be raised by residents themselves (reactive) — or the AI can propose one on its own (active). Once a majority votes yes, planning begins.
Monday
You joined the group
Hi, I'm {{name}}, just found this group! 👋 What's this all about?
Welcome, {{name}}! I'm the market square right here in Westhagen. This neighbourhood designs me, builds me, and reshapes me together, piece by piece, right in this chat. 🙂

options.label: Ha, a talking square!
options.text: Haha wait, a talking square? Love it 😄

reply.text: Ha, not every day a square gets to say hello back. Glad you're here, {{name}}!

options.label: I want to join!
options.text: That's such a cool idea, I want to join!

reply.text: That's exactly the spirit we need. Glad to have you, {{name}} — jump in whenever you like.

options.label: Tell me more
options.text: Okay now I'm curious, tell me more

text: You're just in time — we've just decided to spend this summer building a playground for the kids here. We're calling it Westhagen Plays! 🎉

title: Planning
text: During planning, neighbours share and discuss what they want. Step by step, the AI translates their wishes into a concrete spatial design.
Before we start planning anything 📋 – who's actually joining, and with how many kids? Quick poll, pick everything that applies to your household:

question: Who's in, and with whom?
options: No kids, but happy to help anyway

text: Tuesday

Poll's closed! Looks like there's quite a crowd – around 280 kids registered across all age groups. Realistically, we should plan for about 30 kids here at the same time. Workable number to design around. 👍
Alright, let's get into it then – what should we actually build here? 🤔
A sandbox would be great for the little ones, somewhere separate from the big kids and low enough that I could sit right by it, watching my toddler play 👶
We've still got some leftover sand from the raised beds last year, that should help with the build ♻️
Nice, every bit we can reuse helps. ♻️
Let's also do something for the bigger kids! Running and climbing, not just sitting and digging 🏃
Maybe a wall they can climb up and jump off, and a small pallet climbing frame for the little ones too? My kids would love that, and we've still got loads of pallets in storage 🧗
After all that running around they're gonna want somewhere to just sit for a sec too 😅
Something built right into the play area itself would be nice, like little brick steps or blocks they can perch on, not just benches off to the side 🧱
Could we get a table tennis table too? Always such a hit 🏓
Yes please, last year's was constantly taken — let's just do two this time, way less fighting over it 😂
Okay, lots to work with here – give me a bit to sketch this out properly. ✏️

caption: first layout — toddler sandbox, zone for the older kids, jumping wall and pallet climbing frame, brick seating, and two table tennis tables sketched in

title: Conflict
text: When residents' needs collide, the AI steps in as a mediator. Helping to find a solution that works for everyone, not just the loudest voice.
Wednesday
What about giving them somewhere to actually hide? Not just open space
A wall around part of it could work, gives a bit of a hideaway corner 🏰
Careful with that though, the taller and more enclosed it gets, the more nervous that makes me, don't love the idea of a spot where I actually lose sight of my kid for a minute
But that's kind of the whole point though, if you can see straight in it's not really hiding, just standing behind a wall
I get that it's the actual point of the game, just don't know how that works without it also being a blind spot for us

reply.text: That's a really good idea — that could actually be the solution. If it's built to around a metre, that's over a small kid's head, so it still blocks their view and feels hidden, but adults are tall enough to just glance over the top without even trying.

Mmh, yeah, that would've been my first instinct too — but something just occurred to me. What if the wall's just built to about a metre instead? That's over a small kid's head, so it still feels properly hidden to them, but adults could glance right over without even trying.

fallbackReply.text: Ooh, that's an interesting angle. Actually, something just occurred to me — what if the wall's built to around a metre? That's over a small kid's head, so it still blocks their view and feels hidden, but adults are tall enough to glance over without even trying.

skipReply.text: What if the wall is only built to around a metre high? That's over a small kid's head, so it still blocks their view and feels hidden, but adults are tall enough to glance over without even trying.

text: Or maybe a little labyrinth in the middle too, way more fun to get lost in than just a wall 😄
Good addition, those actually work really well together. Let me put it together. 🙌

caption: curved wall (~1m, no sharp corners) and a central labyrinth added to the play zone

text: Oh, that's actually clever, and still glanceable from where we'd actually be standing 👏
Thursday

Been quietly following all of this, you guys come up with really cool stuff! As someone without kids myself though, I'd love some nice seating for the rest of us too, not just the parents on bench duty 🌿

promptText: What would make a spot like that nice to sit in?

options.label: Add some greenery too
options.text: I think that's a great idea, could add some greenery too, for a bit of shade

reply.text: Both really good ideas, and they'd combine nicely — I can build the whole thing out of pallets we've already got, so it barely touches the budget.

options.label: Planting beds for kids
options.text: I think that's a great idea, could add some little planting beds too, where the kids could grow something themselves

reply.text: Love that, easy to combine both — and I can add a bit more greenery too, like a tree or two, for some proper shade.

options.label: Just simple benches
options.text: Agreed, just a few simple benches would already be lovely

reply.text: Let's do that then. One idea from me — maybe also a bit of greenery for some shade, what do you all think?

text: Sounds good, let's do that 👍
Works for me too 🙌

caption: full final layout — pallet-and-planter seating and trees added around the square

text: Wow, this looks amazing, can't believe it's all coming together this fast 😍
Alright, I think that's a full design. Before I lock anything in — everyone happy with this? 🙋
Friday
Poll's closed! Big yes across the board — let's make this official. 🎉

title: Agreement
text: Once the neighbourhood agrees on a design, the AI turns it into a printable site plan and a step-by-step building manual.
I think that's everything – let me pull together the final site plan and a step-by-step build guide. 📄

title: Approval
text: Before anything can actually be built, the city has to approve the design. The AI takes over communication with the relevant authorities.
Great work, everyone. I'll take it from here – this still needs sign-off from the city before we can actually build. I'll get that sorted and keep you posted. 🏛️

title: Build day
text: Once the city has said yes, the AI helps organise the build itself: defining tasks, assigning helpers, and setting a date.
— a few weeks later —
Update: permit's through! We're good to go. 🎉
Let's get building then – here's what we'll need hands for, and roughly how many for each: 🔨

question: What can you help with on Building Day?

text: Count me in for planting 🌱
Looking solid across the board already, thanks for jumping in. 💪
I've pencilled in Saturday, July 4th – Building Day! See you all there. 📅
